what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view E3 in area E; it declares what the surface represents, which journeys it belongs to, the UI address it builds to, and the components, lint step, and token source used to produce it.
observations: Generated surface-spec for view E3, area E, titled 'Retrieval result — ranked, usable'
Home journey is J2 and journeys list contains only J2; cross_journeys is empty
build_to names the 'inspector' component, a design-lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline
standouts: The title frames the surface as a ranked, usable retrieval result rather than a generic view
The address points to an inspector model-field resource
build_to mixes component, lint gate, and token source in one object
It records that it was built by a Claude Code session with a rule to never invent addresses
conventions: Follows the project surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to
Directory path uses a journey spine (tree) matching home_journey J2
References only existing addresses rather than inventing them
Separates own journeys from cross-journey references
concerns: Status is 'planned', so the spec documents intent rather than an implemented surface
cross_journeys is empty even though the comment mentions carrying the web (B3)
build_to.design_lint is a command string that may be a placeholder or need environment context
Represents value 'MOD-embed' is not explained within the file
notes: A planned, journey-anchored surface spec that maps a ranked retrieval-result view to an inspector UI address and a build pipeline.
questions: What concrete UI element does ui://inspector/model-field/default-brain resolve to?
What does 'MOD-embed' represent in this context?
Where and how is the ranking/usability of retrieval results defined?
What is the B3 web mentioned in the comment and why is cross_journeys empty?
How does the design-lint gate enforce the tokens-only look-source rule?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface spec for a ranked retrieval result; the file matches that purpose as a declarative metadata record, though it is planned rather than implemented.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a planned view surface that presents ranked, usable retrieval results inside an inspector model-field, anchored to journey J2, and produced from a token-based design system with lint validation.
novelty: The surface title explicitly characterizes the view as a 'Retrieval result — ranked, usable' and bundles component, lint gate, and token-source build instructions in the same spec.
contribution: It contributes a planned binding between journey J2, the UI address inspector/model-field/default-brain, and concrete build targets (inspector component, lint gate, token source), giving the system an addressable target for a retrieval-result surface.
summary_for_embedding: E3.json is a planned surface specification for a desktop/mobile ranked retrieval-result view tied to journey J2 and addressed to ui://inspector/model-field/default-brain, with build targets for the inspector component, a design lint gate, and a token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to declare how a ranked, usable retrieval-result surface should be built, validated, and addressed within the J2 journey.
